title: Why is Ayanami Rei a tentative name
display_title: Why is Ayanami Rei a tentative name
wikitext:
[]: Why is Ayanami Rei a tentative name?             Quote Post by UltraB116 » Thu Feb 18, 2021 3:57 am I guess she might have a new name in the last movie? Ikari Rei? Haha I don’t know.

[]: Re: Why is Ayanami Rei a tentative name?             Quote Post by Thesufferingpumpkin » Thu Feb 18, 2021 5:45 am Technically in 3.0 Shinji Ikari is given a tentative name when recovered from unit 01

[]: Re: Why is Ayanami Rei a tentative name?             Quote Post by Heaven Piercing Man » Thu Feb 18, 2021 7:39 am Because she is not Rei Ayanami but we gotta call her something. It's best represented in Japanese. Rei from 1.0 and 2.0 is spelled 綾波レイ, "Ayanami Rei", her family name is in kanji just like everyone else, which carries a meaning. In contrast, Rei Q's name is spelled アヤナミレイ(仮称) which literally translates to "AYANAMIREI (tentative name)", notice how it's just the sounds, no kanji. It's just a name without any meaning beyond the sounds, given to her just because she looks like the real Rei. A designation.

[]: Re: Why is Ayanami Rei a tentative name?             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Feb 18, 2021 9:19 am UltraB116 wrote: I guess she might have a new name in the last movie? Ikari Rei? Haha I don’t know.

[]: Re: Why is Ayanami Rei a tentative name?             Quote Post by BusterMachine4 » Thu Feb 18, 2021 9:57 am What if Shinji and Rei swap their last names? Shinji becomes an Ayanami to associate with his mother rather than his father, and Rei becomes an Ikari to show her connection with Shinji. That weird dream sequence in 1.0 would become one of the most subtle pieces of foreshadowing ever.

[]: Re: Why is Ayanami Rei a tentative name?             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Feb 18, 2021 10:02 am BusterMachine4 wrote: What if Shinji and Rei swap their last names? Shinji becomes an Ayanami to associate with his mother rather than his father, and Rei becomes an Ikari to show her connection with Shinji. That weird dream sequence in 1.0 would become one of the most subtle pieces of foreshadowing ever.

[]: Re: Why is Ayanami Rei a tentative name?             Quote Post by Joseki » Thu Feb 18, 2021 10:04 am Why stop at the surname? Rei Q should decide her own name that is completely unreleted from Ikari, Ayanami and Rei. Moyoco Higuchi maybe

[]: Re: Why is Ayanami Rei a tentative name?             Quote Post by UltraB116 » Fri Feb 19, 2021 4:50 am Thesufferingpumpkin wrote: Technically in 3.0 Shinji Ikari is given a tentative name when recovered from unit 01

[]: Re: Why is Ayanami Rei a tentative name?             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Feb 19, 2021 8:10 pm UltraB116 wrote: Thesufferingpumpkin#910417 wrote: Technically in 3.0 Shinji Ikari is given a tentative name when recovered from unit 01

[]: Re: Why is Ayanami Rei a tentative name?             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Feb 23, 2021 6:32 am ElMariachi wrote: The fandom has been wracking its collective brain trying to figure out what "BM-03" means for nearly nine years now.

[]: Re: Why is Ayanami Rei a tentative name?             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Feb 23, 2021 11:06 am I see it as "Bio Matter", with the suggestion that they've experienced earlier appearances of ... things.

[]: Re: Why is Ayanami Rei a tentative name?             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Tue Feb 23, 2021 4:48 pm I've always figured it was a Tentative Name because at some point in the final film the current iteration of Rei will take ownership of herself and decide on the name she'd like to be called by.

[]: Re: Why is Ayanami Rei a tentative name?             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue Feb 23, 2021 5:21 pm UrsusArctos wrote: Buster Machine 03 appears to be direct reference - from the Black Hole Bomb in Gunbuster. The impact gates that Eva-01 generates under Shinji are themselves Gunbuster references, as seen in the links in the Reddit post below - https://www.reddit.com/r/respectthreads ... gunbuster/ In-universe, I'd guess it's probably "Boy-Male 03" since Shinji's the "Third Boy" in the NTE continuity. They're not even sure that what they've pulled out represents Shinji Ikari as he was prior to dissolving into the Eva, hence the ?? and the "tentative name" business.

[]: Re: Why is Ayanami Rei a tentative name?             Quote Post by UltraB116 » Mon Mar 01, 2021 7:01 pm Shin is coming! I’m really keen to know what is going to happen to ReiQ. I hope she has a good ending, developing her own soul and enjoy her life in the world. If she does has a new name, that’s is possible.

[]: Re: Why is Ayanami Rei a tentative name?             Quote Post by Heaven Piercing Man » Mon Mar 01, 2021 9:55 pm That's why I expect in part. I think Poka Poka Rei is physically gone forever, after all, she WAS supposed to die in 2.0 until relatively late into the storyboarding stage or something like that, let's not forget that narratively she was willing to sacrifice herself so that Shinji wouldn't have to get into the bloody robot ever again (so her arc wasn't "truncated", as some said 8 years ago), and in the end the film ends in a way that she survives but is still allowed to both exit the story as a main character, and persist in a different context (quantum apparitions? Some kind of transcendence?) in a way that doesn't get in the way of Rei Q, who is being set to have her own arc about her identity. I doubt they'll give her a radically different name, though. Maybe she only changes her family name? Rei Ikari anyone? I'm just spitballing.

[]: Re: Why is Ayanami Rei a tentative name?             Quote Post by LightDragonman » Mon Mar 01, 2021 10:47 pm I just really hope Shinji and Rei II get at least closure together, like one last farewell before she disappears to heaven or something. Heck, maybe even have her be the one to tell Rei Q to take care of him and to live for herself and all that jazz. Provided that Anno doesn't let his hatred for her get in the way and just unceremoniously have Shinji and the others forget about her as a way of "rejecting escapism" or some crap.

[]: Re: Why is Ayanami Rei a tentative name?             Quote Post by BusterMachine4 » Tue Mar 02, 2021 12:24 am LightDragonman wrote: Provided that Anno doesn't let his hatred for her get in the way

[]: Re: Why is Ayanami Rei a tentative name?             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Mar 02, 2021 7:24 am Not so much a lie as a misunderstanding of Anno's own statements regarding Rei, most of which had to do with how little he connected to Rei or how he couldn't think of how to develop her emotionally past Episode 06. The very fact that Anno is now giving Rei character development and helping her become more her own person suggests he has her figured out this time around.

[]: Re: Why is Ayanami Rei a tentative name?             Quote Post by UltraB116 » Tue Mar 02, 2021 9:41 am I agree. Rei’s ending is the most mysterious to me. Personally speaking, Shinji, Asuka and Mari are gonna survive after everything happened; Kaworu is probably gonna disappear at the end, since he is one of the Angels. I just don’t know Rei... She might have similar endings like what happened in the old movies and manga; or she could survive too, because ReiQ is different from any Reis in the previous works and she seems to play a big role in Shin. What are your assumptions?

[]: Re: Why is Ayanami Rei a tentative name?             Quote Post by FXArmaros98 » Tue Mar 02, 2021 10:53 am Kaworu is probably gonna disappear at the end, since he is one of the Angels. She might have similar endings like what happened in the old movies and manga; or she could survive too
